nameMeanings.name: Aaron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the brother of Moses, who was appointed by God to be Moses' spokesman and became the first High Priest of the Israelites (Exodus 4:14–16, 7:1–2). It is of uncertain origin and meaning: most probably, like Moses , of Egyptian rather than Hebrew origin. The traditional derivation from Hebrew har-on ‘mountain of strength’ is no more than a folk etymology. The name has been in regular use from time immemorial as a Jewish name and was taken up by the Nonconformists as a Christian name in the 16th century. Since the late 1990s it has been widely popular.

variants.name: Arun
meanings: ‘reddish-brown’, the colour of dawn, gold, and rubies, and personification of the dawn, the charioteer of the sun.

variants.name: Arron
meanings: Variant of either   Aaron   or   Arran .

en.lifeSummary: When Aaron Fitzrogers I was born in 1249, in Roma, Lazio, Italy, his father, Roger Tancred, was 21 and his mother, Irini Angelina, was 21. He had at least 6 sons with Gorrana De Crepon. He died in 1335, in London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 86.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aaron Fitzrogers I родился в 1249 г. в местности рома, лазио, Италия, его отцу по имени Roger Tancred было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Irini Angelina, 21. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей от Gorrana De Crepon. Он умер в 1335 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 86 лет.
